signals.reasoning: The live SOL short has fully round-tripped from a real winner into a deep live loss, retained none of peak PnL, still has no stop, and liquidation distance is now under 1%. This is a hard cleanup exit, not a thesis trade.
BTC has already reclaimed the 72.6K area and the live veto tape now leans relief-style rather than fresh downside continuation, so it is not where to press new bearish exposure.
ETH is participating in the same recovery tape, so it does not offer a cleaner immediate decision than simply flattening the failed SOL short.
TST is too extended and too event-sensitive to trust under a live veto regime.
ORDI is rebounding with the market, but it is not clean enough to justify a fresh call while the priority is cleaning up the open SOL risk.
MERL has constructive recovery structure, but it is still just another rebound name and not a better immediate decision than flattening the failed short.
XPL has liquidity and a rebound, but the live event brake and the failed SOL short both argue against rotating into another aggressive decision right now.
FARTCOIN is the only name with some fresh-shorts-pressing bias mechanically, but forcing a new bearish idea after the SOL short failure is lower quality than standing down.
STABLE still has squeeze fuel, but the setup is too headline-sensitive and too far along to justify touching during a live veto board.
WCT remains interesting on recovery, but execution quality is too messy and the event brake still outweighs the raw bounce.
CHILLGUY still looks more like noisy short-covering than a clean fresh trade, so it is not where disciplined post-exit attention should go.
